items.scenario: You recently signed up for a weekend pottery workshop at the Cedarwood Community Studio. After confirming your registration, you noticed the schedule lists Saturday from 9 AM to 1 PM, but the website still shows an old 10 AM start. You want to clarify the time and ask about parking and what supplies you should bring.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Halverson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the scheduling discrepancy between the confirmation and the website
Ask about parking access and supplies you should bring
Explain that you would appreciate a quick reply before Friday
items.subject: Clarifying Saturday workshop start time and supplies

items.scenario: You are a second-year history major taking a research methods course. After receiving feedback on your first short paper, you are unsure how the professor wants you to handle conflicting primary sources in your final project, which is due in three weeks. You want guidance before you commit to a specific argument.
items.direction: Write an email to Dr. Vasquez. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific conflicting sources you encountered
Ask how the professor recommends handling the disagreement
Suggest a brief meeting during office hours next week
items.subject: Guidance on conflicting sources for the final research paper

items.scenario: You ordered a refurbished electric kettle from an online appliance retailer two weeks ago. The kettle arrived with a damaged handle and an automatic shutoff that no longer works. You paid an extra $12 for express shipping and now want a replacement or refund before your dormitory move-in next month.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Donovan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific defects you discovered on the kettle
Explain the urgency tied to your upcoming dormitory move-in
Request a replacement unit or a full refund including shipping
items.subject: Defective refurbished kettle from order #KT-44218

items.scenario: Your friend Priya proposed driving up to the Willowbrook trailhead this weekend for a two-day camping trip. You are excited but have a Monday morning presentation and need to leave early on Sunday. You also want to coordinate gear so neither of you brings duplicates.
items.direction: Write an email to Priya. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention your excitement about the proposed camping trip
Explain your Sunday departure constraint and the reason
Suggest a quick gear-sharing plan so you avoid duplicates
items.subject: Camping plan and Sunday early departure
